summary: Maintains the technical infrastructure that supports CI‑OS operations, ensuring secure, reliable, and high‑performance IT and OT environments.
responsibilities: Administer IT and OT systems that support CI‑OS workflows, dashboards, automation, and digital twins
Ensure system uptime, network reliability, and secure access control across all CI‑OS components
Manage servers, databases, edge devices, and operational technology interfaces
Monitor system performance, logs, and telemetry to detect anomalies or failures
Implement cybersecurity controls, patches, and vulnerability mitigation strategies
Collaborate with the CI‑OS Architect to deploy integrations, APIs, and data pipelines
Maintain backup, recovery, and business continuity plans
Support deployment of sensors, IoT devices, and telemetry instrumentation
Ensure compliance with IT, OT, and data governance standards
Document system configurations, changes, and operational procedures
authorities: Define access control rules and permissions
kaizenCapabilities: Identification of digital waste and inefficiencies
Support for automation and workflow improvements
Optimization of system performance and reliability
cqiCapabilities: Instrumentation of systems for CQI metrics
Integration of IT/OT data into CQI dashboards

constraints: All changes must comply with cybersecurity and governance standards
System downtime must be minimized and documented
Access control changes require audit logging
